text: The heartbeat evoked potential (HEP) is a brain response measurable on EEG that occurs roughly 300 milliseconds after each heartbeat. Researchers Hyeong-Dong Park and Tali Sharot found that the HEP is stronger in people who are more accurate at detecting their own heartbeats — a proxy for interoceptive awareness. More strikingly, the strength of the HEP predicts how well a person can sustain attention on a given trial. This suggests the brain is not simply reacting to the heart; it is actively listening to it as a pacing signal. Attention and cardiac rhythm are not parallel processes but deeply intertwined ones.
question: What does the strength of the heartbeat evoked potential (HEP) predict, according to the passage?

options.text: How accurately a person can time their breathing to match environmental rhythms
How quickly a person recovers from attentional lapses after distraction
How much cortical gray matter is present in the anterior insula
How well a person can sustain attention on a given trial
